meta_title: For Students: idea validation to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Idea Validation to Solve Debugging Roadblocks
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how to leverage idea validation, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build professional-grade software as a student.
content.answer_block: When you cannot debug, you are often building the wrong thing. Shift from blind coding to rigorous idea validation. By using Antigravity for architectural oversight and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you turn debugging from a wall into a feedback loop that validates your logic before it fails.
content.intro:
You are staring at a terminal full of red stack traces, the silence of your room heavy with the frustration of a project that refuses to compile. Every senior engineer has been exactly where you are: convinced that the next line of code will finally make the logic click, only to find themselves deeper in a labyrinth of technical debt.

The secret isn't better debugging skills; it is better validation. By validating your core idea before writing the implementation, you strip away the unnecessary complexity that breeds bugs. This guide transforms your workflow from reactive fire-fighting into a proactive architecture-first approach.

steps.title: Define the Minimal Viable Logic
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the data flow. If you can't explain the logic in plain English, you can't code it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Architect. Analyze this feature requirement: [Insert Requirement]. Identify the core logical failure points and suggest a state-machine diagram to prevent runtime errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze my current project structure and identify the most likely point of failure in my core data flow.'

steps.title: Validate Assumptions with Mock Data
steps.description: Verify your logic works with static data before connecting to real-world APIs or databases.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit test edge cases for this specific function to ensure it handles null, undefined, and malformed inputs gracefully.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a test file that mocks the API response and validates the function handles empty states.'

steps.title: Automated Code Review
steps.description: Use AI to catch syntax and logic errors before you even try to run the build command.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a senior-level code review on this snippet. Look for race conditions, memory leaks, and poor error handling patterns.

steps.title: Iterative Implementation
steps.description: Build in small, verifiable chunks. Never commit more than 10 lines of code without a successful test run.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this complex block into smaller, pure functions that are easier to debug and test in isolation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Refactor the authentication module into smaller, modular functions.'

steps.title: Root Cause Analysis of Failures
steps.description: When a bug occurs, stop guessing. Use your tools to perform a post-mortem on the specific failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Here is the error log: [Insert Log]. Determine the root cause and provide a fix that prevents this class of error from reoccurring.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'The build failed with this error. Analyze the stack trace and propose a structural fix.'

pro_tips: Treat error messages as documentation, not insults; they are the compiler telling you exactly where your mental model failed.
If you spend more than 30 minutes debugging a single function, delete it and rewrite it from scratch using TDD (Test Driven Development).
Keep a 'Bug Journal' to track patterns in your mistakes; you will realize you make the same three types of errors repeatedly.
Use Claude Code to explain your code back to you; if it misunderstands your intent, your code is likely too complex.
Prioritize 'fail-fast' logic where your app throws meaningful errors early rather than failing silently deep in the stack.
content.next_steps: After mastering these debugging workflows, start contributing to open-source projects to stress-test your architecture against real-world constraints.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs dictate your progress. Master the build process, refine your architectural intuition, and ship projects that actually scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
